If command execution must be gated or disabled by policy, document and implement that as part of the feature rather than bolting it on later.

- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816104Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2771,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"`Match Exec` is one of those features where naïve compatibility can create security regret. The clever implementation is the one that is both honest and safe.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27Z"},{"id":2797,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan

 {"id":"ft-akx00.8","title":"Define shared verification, E2E, and artifact contract for placeholder-remediation work","description":"Define the shared verification contract for the entire `ft-akx00` program so implementers do not improvise test coverage, logging shape, artifact capture, or execution evidence on a bead-by-bead basis.

This bead exists because placeholder-remediation is high risk: many changes touch trust-sensitive control paths, platform backends, or user-facing UX surfaces. We want one reusable contract for proof, not 24 inconsistent interpretations of “tested”.","design":"1. Define the minimum unit-test standard for this program: happy path, edge/boundary behavior, explicit failure/error/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability/idempotence where relevant.
2. Define the E2E/integration contract: each concrete bead must land either a dedicated script or an explicitly named extension to an existing shared suite.
3. Define the structured logging schema for verification runs. Minimum fields: `bead_id`, `scenario_id`, `surface`, `step`, `status`, `duration_ms`, `correlation_id`, `backend`, `platform`, `artifact_dir`, and redaction markers.
4. Define the artifact manifest for every verification run. Minimum files: `commands.txt`, `env.txt`, `structured.log`, `stdout.txt`, `stderr.txt`, `summary.json`, plus surface-specific artifacts like screenshots, pixel diffs, JSON payload snapshots, or remote traces.
5. Define naming conventions for new E2E scripts and artifact directories so failures are easy to browse.
6. Require heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark workloads to run via `rch exec -- ...`, with worker identity and command transcript captured in evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- The shared verification contract is explicit enough that every concrete `ft-akx00.*.*` task can reference it without ambiguity.
- Proposed E2E suite names and artifact directory conventions exist for every track in the program.
- The contract includes structured logging, artifact capture, redaction, and reproduction-command requirements.
- The contract is reflected back into the existing `ft-akx00` epics/tasks via updated acceptance criteria, notes, or comments.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.480153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.479984Z","close_reason":"Completed shared verification contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2773,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Suggested suite map for future implementers:
- machine-control/client/sdk -> `scripts/e2e_machine_control_*.sh`
- operator/startup/backup -> `scripts/e2e_ops_*.sh`
- connector/search -> `scripts/e2e_search_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_connector_*.sh`
- core/FTUI -> `scripts/e2e_core_*.sh` and TUI snapshot fixtures
- rendering -> `scripts/e2e_render_*.sh` plus screenshot/pixel-diff artifacts
- mux/SSH -> `scripts/e2e_mux_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_ssh_*.sh`

Completion evidence for any future bead under this epic should be considered incomplete unless it records exact commands, artifact paths, and enough logs to diagnose failure without rerunning locally.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25Z"},{"id":2821,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Progress update: tightened the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` and the owned ft-akx00 harnesses for 6.2 / 6.4. The concrete changes are: 1) explicit probe/smoke log path helpers, 2) extractor helpers for selected worker / probe worker inventory / sync duration / remote duration / exit code, 3) a sentinel smoke log when `RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1`, and 4) owned harness summaries now record `rch_probe`, `rch_smoke`, and per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars. Verified this contract concretely with `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_2_backend_resize_framebuffer.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.2/backend_resize_framebuffer/20260403T182413Z`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:28:32Z"},{"id":2822,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Follow-up validation: the tightened contract was also exercised via `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_4_terminfo_image_rendering.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.4/terminfo_image_rendering/20260403T182902Z`. That run produced a complete `summary.json` plus `rch_probe` / `rch_smoke` / per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars even on failure. Results were 3 pass / 1 fail; the failing leg was remote clippy, and the failure was unrelated repo lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs` (`clippy::collapsible_match`), not a break in the contract plumbing itself. This gives the ft-akx00 contract both an honest failure-path example and a passing-step example with captured worker identity and durations.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:36:45Z"},{"id":3524,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"SunnyTiger","text":"SunnyTiger 2026-04-19: standardized the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in repo docs. Canonical references are now `docs/test-logging-contract.md` (new `ft-akx00` profile with required JSONL fields, artifact set, and reproduction-command rules) and `docs/e2e-harness-spec.md` (new bead-scoped verification profile, required artifact root, suite map for tracks 2-7, and redaction/evidence requirements). Also extended `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` metadata sidecars to record `repo_root`, `smoke_target_dir`, timeout settings, and whether smoke preflight was explicitly requested to be skipped, so the docs match auditable on-disk evidence instead of chat-only expectations.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:32:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-al88s","title":"connector governor quota lifetime counters count rejected attempts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:05.045975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:50.738331Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:50.737983Z","close_reason":"Verified in 946c9422; exact fix landed and local proof was blocked only by unrelated frankenterm-core compile failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-ao9k9","title":"[mock-finder] Workflow runner treats external waits as timeout sleeps","description":"Production defect found by mock-code-finder in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:67-71 handles WaitCondition::External by calling wait_duration_maybe_cx(timeout), so a workflow external wait sleeps until timeout instead of observing an external signal.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs:223-290 has the real ExternalSignalRegistry-backed executor, but this runner path does not wire a registry into workflow execution.

Impact:
Workflows using WaitCondition::External cannot unblock when the external signal fires through the event-driven runner; they wait the whole timeout and can make signal-based coordination look hung.

Expected fix:
Thread ExternalSignalRegistry through WorkflowRunner execution, or route runner wait conditions through WaitConditionExecutor with a registry. Add a regression where an external signal satisfies before timeout and a missing registry fails explicitly.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:07:18.681859Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:14:31.236729Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-ao9k9","title":"[mock-finder] Workflow runner treats external waits as timeout sleeps","description":"Production defect found by mock-code-finder in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:67-71 handles WaitCondition::External by calling wait_duration_maybe_cx(timeout), so a workflow external wait sleeps until timeout instead of observing an external signal.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs:223-290 has the real ExternalSignalRegistry-backed executor, but this runner path does not wire a registry into workflow execution.

Impact:
Workflows using WaitCondition::External cannot unblock when the external signal fires through the event-driven runner; they wait the whole timeout and can make signal-based coordination look hung.

Expected fix:
Thread ExternalSignalRegistry through WorkflowRunner execution, or route runner wait conditions through WaitConditionExecutor with a registry. Add a regression where an external signal satisfies before timeout and a missing registry fails explicitly.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:07:18.681859Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:44:55.426008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.105111Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.104627Z","close_reason":"Already fixed — all three panic messages (header doc, missing-golden, drift) consistently use --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime after f6e7cc15 and subsequent commits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-atcr0","title":"backpressure::classify silently returns Green when config thresholds are NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/backpressure.rs` at `BackpressureManager::classify` (lines 416-438):

```rust
if capture_saturated || write_saturated {
    BackpressureTier::Black
} else if cr >= self.config.red_capture || wr >= self.config.red_write {
    BackpressureTier::Red
} else if cr >= self.config.yellow_capture || wr >= self.config.yellow_write {
    BackpressureTier::Yellow
} else {
    BackpressureTier::Green
}
```

Any `>=` comparison with NaN is false. If the operator ships a BackpressureConfig where one or more thresholds is NaN (TOML supports `yellow_capture = nan`, `red_capture = nan`, etc.), every branch falls through to Green. The backpressure monitor reports 'healthy' regardless of actual queue depth — disabling every upgrade action (pane-pause, detection skipping, capacity shedding).

# Parallel to ft-761tz

This is the same shape as c5a02edb (ft-761tz, disk_pressure):
- disk_pressure `classify_tier` silently returned Green on NaN usage_fraction — fixed by fail-closed Black return at the top of classify_tier.
- EwmaEstimator / PidController silently propagated NaN through clamp — fixed by NaN guard at update.

backpressure.rs has not received the same hardening. Config thresholds are `f64` and deserialized via serde; TOML's `nan` literal slips through unchanged. No `validate()` method exists on BackpressureConfig.

# Fix

1. Add a `BackpressureConfig::validate()` method that rejects non-finite thresholds, negative values, and yellow ≥ red misordering. Emit a descriptive error.
2. Defense-in-depth inside `classify`: if ANY relevant config threshold or input ratio is non-finite, return `BackpressureTier::Black` to fail closed (same disposition as ft-761tz).
3. Regression tests:
   - config_validate_rejects_nan_yellow_capture
   - config_validate_rejects_red_below_yellow
   - classify_returns_black_on_nan_config_threshold (defense-in-depth path)

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires operator misconfiguration to trigger, but the failure mode is silent (no panic, no error) and the blast radius is 'backpressure is effectively disabled' which can let the watcher runaway into OOM under load. The ft-761tz fix landed as HIGH; applying the same shape here keeps the fleet's failure model consistent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:03:47.755984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773267Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): NaN-proof backpressure)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Observed failure:

- command: `rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p8-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib classify_small_write_capacity_requires_high_fill_for_black`
- worker: `vmi1149989`

- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:12:32.293332Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:33:48.871320Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:33:48.870997Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 31bad457","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -222,7 +222,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-dqsev","title":"[bug] delete_session runs 3 DELETEs without a transaction — partial failure yields orphaned checkpoints","description":"# Bug

\`session_restore::delete_session\` at \`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:1003\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

Three independent DELETEs, each in its own implicit rusqlite
transaction. If step 1 succeeds but step 2 fails (lock contention,
disk full, IO error, cancellation), we leave the database in a
tornn-inconsistent state:

  - Option A: pane_states DELETED, checkpoints still present,
    session still present → \`load_checkpoint_by_id\` returns
    Some(checkpoint) but with empty \`pane_states\` → restore
    produces an empty-pane session that looks clean but carries no
    terminal state.

  - Option B: pane_states + checkpoints DELETED, session row
    still present → \`list_sessions\` shows the session, but
    \`load_latest_checkpoint\` returns None. Next \`delete_session\`
    call's third DELETE succeeds → session is eventually cleaned
    up, but the window is observable.

The other deletion-style paths in session_restore.rs DO wrap their
mutations in \`conn.transaction()\` (see lines 626, 673). delete_session
is the anomaly.

# Fix

Wrap the three DELETEs in a single \`conn.transaction()\` that
commits on success or rolls back on any intermediate error.

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let mut conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    let tx = conn.transaction()?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    tx.commit()?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

# Test

Regression test: inject mid-operation failure (e.g., by DELETING a
session that references a checkpoint whose pane_state rows have a
trigger or hook that fails). Without a transaction, the partial
state is observable; with the fix, the session row remains.

For a simpler test, assert that after a successful delete_session
there are zero rows in all three tables for the target session_id,
AND that commit/rollback atomicity holds across a wrapping PRAGMA.

# Scope

Single function, ~6 LOC change. No schema migration. Preserves the
return value contract (true if session row was deleted).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:06.907980Z","created_by":"pane3-delete-tx","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944608Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1a090629. Wrapped delete_session's three DELETE statements in a single conn.transaction() with commit on success. Pattern lifted from existing transactional helpers at session_restore.rs:626 + :673. Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl pre-existing breakage — change is mechanical and preserves the return-value contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomicity","session","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910506Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 26841c83: build.rs now uses .ok().filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty()) so SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='' and whitespace-only values collapse to None (fallback to wall-clock). Some('0') preserved as a valid epoch. Code comment documents the rationale. Build.rs has no cargo-test-reachable helpers, so no unit test added; verified via standalone rustc --edition 2024 parse + runtime dry-run on ['', '   ', '0', '1700000000', None].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-dvgzi","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs: 2058-line "e2e" suite uses MockWezterm — should drive a real WeztermClient subprocess for snapshot/restore","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs (2058 lines) is named e2e but
substitutes MockWezterm for the real pane mux at every test entry point:
  20: use frankenterm_core::wezterm::{MockWezterm, ...};
  752, 1073, 1302, 1599, 1773, 2017: let wezterm = Arc::new(MockWezterm::new());

## Why the mock is risky
Snapshot/restore is the durability contract for pane state: scrollback
bytes, layout tree, cursor, selection, IME composition, etc. With a mock
wezterm the assertions verify "we wrote some bytes" rather than "a freshly-
spawned wezterm pane restored to the snapshotted state matches the
original frame-for-frame." The 2026-04-26 e2e_replay-related failures and
the ft-1memj.28 split-divider geometry pin are exactly the category of
bug the mock cannot surface.

Mock Risk score: 5 (data: lose pane state) × 5 (MockWezterm dramatically
simpler than real WeztermClient) = 25 → MUST be mock-free per the no-mocks
skill matrix.

## Real fixture available
crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:859 defines `WeztermClient`
(pub struct, real implementation) and line 2606 `impl WeztermInterface
for WeztermClient`. A test-time fixture spawning `wezterm-mux-server`
(or a thin headless equivalent) via std::process::Command and
attaching WeztermClient is feasible. tests/common/lab.rs already
hosts the harness scaffolding.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `WeztermSubprocessFixture` to tests/common/lab.rs that spawns a
   real wezterm-mux-server + WeztermClient pair, exposes a
   `WeztermHandle`, and tears down the subprocess on Drop.
2. Migrate ONE snapshot_e2e test at a time to the real fixture.
3. Keep the MockWezterm path for the ~50% of tests that probe purely
   layout/protocol logic (those score < 8 and are fine to mock).
4. Land structured JSON-line logging (phase=spawn|snapshot|restore|verify,
   ms, byte counts, frame deltas) so CI failures are debuggable.
5. Pin the migration with a regression test: take a snapshot of pane
   with non-trivial scrollback (10k lines) + cursor mid-OSC + active
   selection, restart the mux subprocess, restore from snapshot, diff
   the restored frame against the original.

## Acceptance
- ≥3 snapshot_e2e tests run against a real wezterm-mux subprocess.
- Diff between original and restored frame ≤ comparator tolerance.
- No regression: existing MockWezterm tests still green.

## Out of scope
- Migrating the entire 2058-line file (incremental).
- Modifying WeztermClient or MockWezterm themselves.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:17.294418Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:31.177776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","session-persistence","snapshot","test"],"comments":[{"id":3655,"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi","author":"jemanuel","text":"[cc_2 progress note — fixture landed at 3598a9618; full migration deferred]

## Shipped
WeztermSubprocessFixture in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/common/wezterm_subprocess.rs
plus a 3-test smoke suite at tests/wezterm_subprocess_smoke.rs. Foundation
work that ft-2funa also needs.

The fixture spawns a hermetic wezterm-mux-server with TempDir-backed HOME,
returns a real WeztermClient::with_socket(...), and tears down on Drop.
Verified locally: FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 cargo test passes 3/3 (real mux
subprocess, list_panes returns the auto-spawned pane).

## Why the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs migration is NOT in this commit
The bead's headline ("convert to real WeztermSubprocessFixture") is bigger
than 1 hour. Surfacing concrete structural blockers so the next agent can
budget correctly:

1. **Pane-id semantics differ.** Every snapshot_e2e test uses MockWezterm's
   `add_default_pane(pane_id)` to seed caller-chosen IDs (e.g. 7, 11, 42).
   The real mux assigns pane IDs at spawn time. The smoke test confirms
   the auto-spawned default_prog pane is `pane_id=0`. Migrating means
   reworking each test to discover the assigned ID after spawn, not
   hard-code one — that's a per-test rewrite, not a search-and-replace.

2. **Output injection model is incompatible.** MockWezterm exposes
   `inject_output(pane, text)` for direct buffer manipulation. The real
   mux has no equivalent: output comes from the spawned shell's PTY.
   To produce deterministic content we'd need a custom shell program
   (e.g. spawn `/bin/sh -c 'printf "..."; sleep 600'`), and the test
   has to wait for the renderer to flush — async stabilization that the
   mock sidesteps entirely.

3. **Restore semantics assume process survival.** The tests at
   snapshot_e2e.rs:755, 1076, 1305, 1601, 1778, 2020 all do
   `restorer.restore(&session, &checkpoint, wezterm.clone())` using
   ONE Arc handle for both snapshot AND restore phases. The real e2e
   shape is "snapshot, kill mux, restart mux, restore against the
   fresh subprocess." Migrating means actually restarting the
   subprocess between phases and pointing a fresh WeztermClient at the
   new socket — significant scaffolding the current LayoutRestorer API
   probably doesn't support cleanly.

4. **Frame stabilization.** Real renderer is async; tests need to
   wait for steady state before snapshotting (vs MockWezterm which
   is synchronous in-memory). Need a `wait_until_idle(timeout)`
   helper on top of the fixture.

## Recommended next steps
- ft-dvgzi.1 (P2): Author 3-5 NEW snapshot tests built from the fixture
  upward (no mock baseline to migrate). Cover the easy cases first:
  one-pane snapshot+list, two-pane layout snapshot, restart-and-list.
  This proves the e2e contract on real infrastructure without fighting
  the existing 2058 lines.
- ft-dvgzi.2 (P3): Migrate snapshot_e2e.rs piecemeal, one test at a
  time, only after ft-dvgzi.1 surfaces the API gaps in LayoutRestorer.
- ft-2funa: now unblocked for the fixture portion; fault-injection
  helpers (.stop_pane, .pause_socket, .latency_inject) are still needed.

Leaving ft-dvgzi IN_PROGRESS so the .1 / .2 children can attach.","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-dvgzi.2","title":"[ft-dvgzi.2] piecemeal migrate snapshot_e2e.rs MockWezterm tests to real WeztermSubprocessFixture","description":"## Goal
After ft-dvgzi.1 surfaces the API gaps in LayoutRestorer (probably
needs a way to point at a fresh mux subprocess after restart), migrate
the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs file ONE TEST AT A TIME to use the
WeztermSubprocessFixture.

## Why piecemeal
Each test in snapshot_e2e.rs uses MockWezterm's caller-chosen pane IDs
and synchronous output injection. Reworking each test requires:
- Discover real pane IDs post-spawn instead of hard-coding
- Use a deterministic shell program for content (not inject_output)
- Restart the mux subprocess between snapshot and restore phases
- Wait for renderer steady state before assertions

That's a per-test rewrite, not a mechanical replacement. P3 because
ft-dvgzi.1 delivers the no-mocks coverage value; this is migration
debt.

## Acceptance
- All 8+ snapshot_e2e tests run against real mux OR remain on
  MockWezterm with an explicit comment justifying why (e.g.,
  state-machine-only tests that score < 8 in the no-mocks Mock Risk
  matrix).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","snapshot","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi.2","depends_on_id":"ft-dvgzi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi.2","depends_on_id":"ft-dvgzi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e018k","title":"event_identity_key nested Object insertion order leaks into hash (recursive fix for 765743d5)","description":"765743d5 sorted top-level keys of extracted object, but nested Object/Array values went through serde_json::to_string which preserves IndexMap insertion order. Two logically-equivalent events with nested {'context': {'a':1,'b':2}} vs {'context': {'b':2,'a':1}} hashed differently → dedup-to-two. Fix landed in 50ff1eb0: canonicalize_json_value recursively sorts all nested Object keys before serialization (arrays stay ordered per JSON spec, but their contained Objects get canonicalized). Regression tests event_identity_key_ignores_nested_extracted_object_insertion_order + event_identity_key_canonicalizes_objects_inside_arrays.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.936724Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227773Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227512Z","close_reason":"Landed in 50ff1eb0 (fix(events): recursively canonicalize nested Object keys in normalized_extracted)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-o657v","title":"conformance_mcp_manifest: pin tool input_schema additionalProperties:false invariant","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` rejects unknown top-level fields (via `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`), checks uniqueness of tool/resource/prompt names, and validates minimal/optional field presence. But it does NOT pin a security-relevant invariant that every shipped MCP tool CURRENTLY satisfies: **every tool's `input_schema` has `additionalProperties: false`**.

Verified by:

    $ rg -c 'additionalProperties' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29
    $ rg -c 'input_schema:' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29

All 29 input_schemas currently set it. Without a test pinning this, a future tool author could add a new tool without the clause and the server would silently accept extra fields from untrusted MCP clients — side-channel surface for policy bypass, audit log pollution, or ambiguous-field attacks on tools that later try to evolve their schema.

# Fix

Add `every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties` in conformance_mcp_manifest.rs that iterates `server.tools()` from `build_server_with_db(&Config::default(), Some(...))` and asserts each `tool.input_schema` is a JSON object with `"additionalProperties": false` at the top level. If a tool's schema is NOT type=object (e.g. `{}`, or a primitive), flag that too — all MCP tools in this server should accept structured object arguments.

# Non-fix

Middleware-injected `format` property (from augment_tool_schema_with_format in mcp_middleware.rs) is a known accepted exception because it's stripped from arguments before the inner call. The test should assert additionalProperties:false; the format injection happens to the properties map, not to additionalProperties, so the test passes cleanly.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect; every current tool sets the bit. But regression risk is real as new tools land, and a silently-missing additionalProperties:false turns into a real policy bypass if the tool ever evolves its allowed field set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:03:57.448008Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683105Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of f73e139a (absorbed commit). every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties iterates server.tools() from build_server_with_db + asserts each input_schema is type=object with additionalProperties literal false — pins the 29/29 invariant so future tools without the clause break CI instead of silently widening the accepted field set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"ft-of71n","title":"[mock-finder] Contract artifact bundle emits non-Rust SDK transport stubs","description":"Production defect found by mock-code-finder in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs:1708-1722 standard_contract_artifacts inserts Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go SDK sources into the exported ContractArtifactBundle.
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs:970-989 renders the Python client with _call raising NotImplementedError("transport not wired"). The TypeScript and Go renderers have equivalent transport-not-wired stubs, and SdkLanguage::is_fully_supported only returns true for Rust.

Impact:
Machine-contract/release artifact bundles include plausible SDK source files for Python/TypeScript/Go even though their transports are intentionally not wired. Downstream consumers can treat those files as generated client artifacts rather than skeletons.

Expected fix:
Either emit only fully supported SDK languages from standard_contract_artifacts, or split skeleton templates into a clearly labeled separate artifact with metadata that marks them unsupported. Add a regression that standard release/audit bundles contain no transport-not-wired stubs unless explicitly requested.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:08:52.622647Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:08:52.622647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-ojgmo","title":"[deadlock] workflow runner cx-cancel can strand on idle event subscriber","description":"File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:769-790; crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:805-810,2157-2170; crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:2159-2167

Repro narrative:
1. Start WorkflowRunner::run_with_cx(...) on an idle EventBus with no detection events flowing.
2. Let the loop pass the top-of-loop cx.checkpoint() and park inside subscriber.recv_cx(cx).
3. Cancel the caller Cx after the recv is already suspended.
4. The underlying asupersync broadcast recv does not register a cancel waker mid-flight, so the parked recv is not re-woken by cancel alone.
5. runtime_compat::broadcast::Receiver::recv_with_cx also collapses Cancelled into Closed, so cancel and bus shutdown are conflated once an external wake finally occurs.
6. Result: the workflow runner shutdown can hang until an unrelated event arrives or the bus closes, which is a real cancellation-stranding bug in the async control path.

Expected: recv_cx should surface Cancelled promptly enough for run_with_cx to stop without waiting for unrelated traffic, and cancel should not be reported as Closed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:38.816705Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:02:09.560010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:02:09.559655Z","close_reason":"Landed in fa1fbffe: event subscriber and runner now surface RecvError::Cancelled and race idle broadcast waits against cx cancel. Targeted verification was partially blocked by RCH worker clang-path mismatch and a long local frankenterm-core rebuild.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-okhhj","title":"perf(storage): split FTS rank query from snippet/highlight hydration","description":"Hot path: crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15762-15779 selects full s.content plus snippet(...) and highlight(...) for every FTS result row before LIMIT hydration, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15797-15810 orders by bm25 then maps all selected fields. Broad queries over large panes force FTS5 to construct full highlighted content even when callers only need snippets or IDs.

Concrete optimization: make search_fts_with_snippets a two-stage query. First SELECT rowid,bm25(output_segments_fts) ORDER BY score LIMIT ? with filters, then hydrate only the limited rowids from output_segments and compute snippet/highlight only when options request them. Also add an option/default path that omits highlight() unless a caller explicitly needs full highlighted content. Add an integration perf assertion or trace counter for broad MATCH terms to prove fewer bytes/materialized strings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"amber-fox","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:33:17.375290Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:18:21.461528Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:18:21.461365Z","close_reason":"Two-stage FTS path shipped in 6a0590d2 (parallel session) — search_fts_with_snippets now splits into search_fts_rank_stage + search_fts_hydrate_stage so snippet()/highlight() compute only for the top-N ids, not for every matching candidate. SearchOptions.include_highlights toggle (default mirrors include_snippets) lets callers skip the highlight() call entirely while keeping snippets. Regression tests pinned in 76d82223: fts_search_skips_highlight_when_disabled_but_keeps_snippet (verifies SQL build-time toggle) + fts_search_two_stage_preserves_ordering (verifies rank-then-hydrate restitch preserves BM25 ASC, captured_at ASC, id ASC ordering). All 16 storage::fts_search_* tests pass under no-default-features.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
